methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Ouros Energy is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $3M to date. The valuation analysis employs two methods triangulated against a peer universe of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables in Climate & Energy, supplemented by reference to a broader set of 8 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

The Stage-Progression method applies a typical 2.5x multiplier to the $3M total raised, implying a valuation of $8M. The Peer-Median method references three seed/Series A stage companies: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), yielding a median valuation of $150M with a range of $110M to $200M. These two methods diverge substantially, reflecting the wide dispersion common in early-stage venture valuations where comparable company multiples are highly sensitive to stage definition and market conditions.

Triangulation of the two methods produces a low-end valuation of $6M, a median of $79M, and a high-end of $172M. The wide range reflects genuine methodological tension: Stage-Progression anchors to observable capital raised, while Peer-Median captures market pricing at more advanced stages. Key uncertainties include whether peer multiples at Series A are appropriate guides to seed-stage pricing, the degree to which Ouros's technology differentiation justifies peer comparables, and whether capital raised is an accurate proxy for current equity value.
